prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arrr, ye be wonderin’ why the sky be blue, eh? Pull up a barrel and lend me yer ear, matey! ‘Tis all to do with that great burning orb in the sky—the sun herself—an’ the way her light dances through the air like a fleet o’ ships in a storm. Sunlight, ye see, be made o’ many colors, blendin’ together like the treasures in a pirate king’s chest.

But as that light breaches the wide open sea o’ the heavens, it smashes into the tiny bits o’ air floatin’ about. The blue part o’ the light, bein’ scrappier and lighter than the rest, gets tossed about from one air-mate to another, scatterin’ every which way. An’ that, me heartie, is why the whole upper world glows blue afore our very eyes.

So next time ye look to the blue yonder, think o’ it as the sky’s way o’ wavin’ the pirate flag—bright and bold—thanks to the sun’s light and the air’s mischief! Yarrr!
